description_long: This object is a realistic representation of a crab, featuring a broad, oval-shaped carapace with a slightly domed profile and subtle mottled patterns in shades of beige and brown. The crab has two prominent, asymmetrical claws (chelae) at the front, with one claw noticeably larger than the other, and four pairs of segmented walking legs extending from the sides. The surface of the carapace is hard and slightly rough, with visible joints and subtle textural details on the legs and claws. The underside is not visible, but the overall form suggests a typical decapod crustacean structure. The eyes are small and positioned at the front corners of the carapace, but antennae are not clearly visible in these views.
description: A beige-brown crab with a domed carapace, two large claws, and eight segmented legs, featuring a hard, slightly rough surface.
description_view_0: Front view showing the broad carapace, both claws, and the symmetrical arrangement of the legs.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the larger claw and the segmentation of the walking legs.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full width of the carapace and the spread of the legs.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view emphasizing the smaller claw and the curvature of the carapace.
